title: 2 Boston men accused of assaulting man who hit their car while trying to park
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Anthony Shearrion, 41, and Arkie Swinson, 45, are charged with assault after a parking incident in Dorchester.
The suspects allegedly followed the victim, smashed his car window, and beat him, witnesses reported to police.
The victim sustained non-life-threatening injuries and both suspects were held without bail pending an April 14 court date.

title: 2026 Red Sox preview: 4 questions facing Alex Cora’s club
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Red Sox enter the 2026 season with questions about lineup strength after missing key free-agent signings.
Boston's bullpen, led by Chapman and Whitlock, seeks to replicate its 2025 success despite potential challenges.
Improvements are sought in infield defense, with new acquisitions Contreras and Durbin aiming to reduce errors.

title: Healey announces kickoff of public safety initiative for 2026 World Cup
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey announced Massachusetts' safety plan for 2026 World Cup matches at Gillette Stadium.
The initiative, called 'Match-Ready Massachusetts,' is funded by $76 million in federal security funds.
Special event trains and traffic adjustments are planned to manage increased crowds and transportation needs.

title: Kraft-owned New England Revolution backing White Stadium renovation
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: The Kraft-owned New England Revolution supports the $135 million White Stadium renovation in Franklin Park, Boston.
Boston Legacy FC will invest $190 million into the project, with $252 million committed in community benefits over 15 years.
The Franklin Park Defenders lawsuit claims the stadium development violates Massachusetts Constitution Article 97 regarding public parkland.

title: Maine man in critical condition after stopping to help stranded driver
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 54-year-old Medford man is in critical condition after being struck by a car on I-95 in Veazie, Maine.
The incident occurred when he stopped to help an 18-year-old stranded Bangor driver on a snowy Monday night.
A 19-year-old Old Town driver lost control and hit them; both drivers sustained minor injuries and were hospitalized.

title: Berkshire County man arrested after suspicious early-morning fire in his home
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Monterey fire officials suspect John Rohane, 60, intentionally set a fire at his 340 Main Road residence.
State Police arrested Rohane after finding him in the nearby Beartown State Forest on Wednesday evening.
No injuries were reported, and Rohane was taken to a hospital for issues unrelated to the fire or his arrest.

title: Lured by pepperoni, dog rescued from 12-foot backyard sinkhole on Cape Cod
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Falmouth Fire and Rescue crews rescued an 11-year-old Labrador named Tessa from a 12-foot sinkhole on Cape Cod.
The sinkhole, deemed unsafe for entry, likely formed due to a leak in an underground pipe, officials reported.
Rescuers used pepperoni to lure Tessa closer, then extracted her with a pole leash; she was unharmed.

title: New England-based rescue takes in batch of neglected axolotls
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Libertyland Axolotl Rescue in New England is rehabilitating 16 axolotls with missing limbs and infections from overcrowding.
The organization, specializing in axolotls, names its rescues after cheeses and seeks donations for veterinary care costs.
Founded in 2024, the nonprofit has successfully rehomed over 100 axolotls and educates the public on their care.

title: The beauty of a few cracks
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Meredith Goldstein explores how imperfections, like dents and burn marks, symbolize friendship, growth, and love in relationships.
Goldstein shares a story of Yowei Shaw's burnt table as a lesson in hiding loved ones and personal growth.
Love Letters invites readers to share stories of damage representing love and community, emphasizing a philosophical acceptance of imperfections.

title: Waltham High opens student-run salon for real-world cosmetology training
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Waltham High School opened a 25-station student-run salon for hands-on cosmetology training.
Students can gain 1,000 training hours, qualifying them for the state cosmetology licensing exam.
The salon, offering public services under instructor supervision, enhances real-world experience for students.
